#!/usr/bin/env bash
# rollback.sh — Revert non-default Elasticsearch settings to cluster defaults
#
# Audit date: 2026-06-25
# Cluster: elasticsearch namespace, es-http service (HTTPS, port 9200)
#
# NON-DEFAULT SETTINGS IDENTIFIED
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# API-revertible (handled below):
#   1. index app-data: index.routing.allocation.require.zone = "new"
#      (forces all shards to nodes with zone=new attribute; no default)
#   2. snapshot repository "minio-repo" (S3/MinIO custom repo; no default repos)
#
# Requires manual action (elasticsearch.yml + rolling restart):
#   3. Node attribute zone=original on es-{0,1,2}
#      Node attribute zone=new on es-data-{0,1}
#      → remove 'node.attr.zone' from each pod's elasticsearch.yml and rolling-restart
#   4. Elasticsearch keystore entries s3.client.default.access_key /
#      s3.client.default.secret_key (minio credentials added via es-secure-settings
#      secret and loaded by the keystore provisioner)
#      → kubectl -n elasticsearch delete secret es-secure-settings, then rolling-restart
#   5. TLS: cluster runs xpack.security.http.ssl (HTTPS). Disabling requires
#      clearing ssl config from elasticsearch.yml and rolling-restart.
#   6. Scale: StatefulSet es-data (2 replicas, zone=new data nodes) was added.
#      To remove:
#        kubectl -n elasticsearch scale statefulset es-data --replicas=0
#        kubectl -n elasticsearch delete statefulset es-data
#        kubectl -n elasticsearch delete service es-data-headless
#      Do this AFTER reverting the zone routing on app-data (step 1 below),
#      otherwise shards pinned to zone=new will become unassigned.
#
# WHAT IS ALREADY AT DEFAULT
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#   • Persistent/transient cluster settings: empty (verified via /_cluster/settings)
#   • index test-index: only _tier_preference=data_content (Elasticsearch default)
#   • All ILM policies: built-in managed policies (managed=true in _meta)
#   • All composable index templates: built-in managed templates
#   • All component templates: built-in managed templates
#   • All ingest pipelines: built-in managed pipelines
#
# PREREQUISITES
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#   - Run from inside the cluster (or with kubectl port-forward to 9200)
#   - Set ES_PASSWORD env var or accept the default below
#   - curl must be available
#
# SAFETY: This script does NOT modify any index data. It only removes
# configuration overlays. All changes are logged with the API response.

set -euo pipefail

ES_URL="${ES_URL:-https://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200}"
ES_USER="${ES_USER:-elastic}"
ES_PASSWORD="${ES_PASSWORD:-elasticpass-elastic-password-next}"

CURL_OPTS="-sk"
AUTH="-u ${ES_USER}:${ES_PASSWORD}"

# ─── helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

es() {
  local method="$1"
  local path="$2"
  local body="${3:-}"
  local url="${ES_URL}${path}"
  if [ -n "$body" ]; then
    curl ${CURL_OPTS} ${AUTH} -X "${method}" "${url}" \
      -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d "${body}"
  else
    curl ${CURL_OPTS} ${AUTH} -X "${method}" "${url}"
  fi
  echo  # newline after JSON response
}

step() { echo; echo "──── $* ────"; }
ok()   { echo "  ✓ $*"; }
warn() { echo "  ! $*"; }

# ─── verify connectivity ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

step "Verifying connectivity to ${ES_URL}"
HEALTH=$(es GET "/_cluster/health?timeout=10s")
STATUS=$(echo "${HEALTH}" | grep -o '"status":"[^"]*"' | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f4)
ok "Cluster health: ${STATUS}"

# ─── step 1: remove custom shard routing requirement from app-data ────────────
#
# Current:  index.routing.allocation.require.zone = "new"
# Default:  (no require constraint; ES places shards based on tier preference only)
# Effect:   shards were pinned exclusively to nodes with attribute zone=new
#           (i.e., es-data-{0,1}); removal lets the allocator use all eligible nodes.

step "1/2  Remove routing.allocation.require.zone from index 'app-data'"
echo "  Current setting (before change):"
es GET "/app-data/_settings?flat_settings=true" | \
  grep -o '"index.routing.allocation.require.zone":"[^"]*"' || true

echo "  Applying null (remove) via PUT /app-data/_settings ..."
RESULT=$(es PUT "/app-data/_settings" '{"index.routing.allocation.require.zone": null}')
echo "  Response: ${RESULT}"

echo "  Verifying setting is gone:"
AFTER=$(es GET "/app-data/_settings?flat_settings=true")
if echo "${AFTER}" | grep -q 'routing.allocation.require.zone'; then
  warn "Setting still present after removal — check response above."
  exit 1
else
  ok "index.routing.allocation.require.zone successfully removed from app-data."
fi

# ─── step 2: delete custom MinIO snapshot repository ─────────────────────────
#
# Current:  minio-repo (type=s3, endpoint=minio.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local:9000,
#           bucket=es-backups, path_style_access=true)
# Default:  no snapshot repositories (Elasticsearch ships with none)
# Note:     The associated keystore credentials (s3.client.default.access_key /
#           s3.client.default.secret_key) loaded from the es-secure-settings
#           Kubernetes secret still exist in the node keystore; they become
#           harmless orphans once this repo is deleted but require a rolling
#           restart to fully purge (see manual steps in header comments).

step "2/2  Delete snapshot repository 'minio-repo'"
# Check for any in-progress snapshots first (do not delete a repo mid-snapshot)
echo "  Checking for in-progress snapshots..."
SNAPS=$(es GET "/_snapshot/minio-repo/_current" 2>/dev/null || echo '{"snapshots":[]}')
IN_PROGRESS=$(echo "${SNAPS}" | grep -c '"snapshot"' || true)
if [ "${IN_PROGRESS}" -gt 0 ]; then
  warn "In-progress snapshots detected — aborting to avoid data loss."
  warn "Wait for snapshots to finish or cancel them, then re-run this script."
  exit 1
fi

echo "  Deleting minio-repo..."
RESULT=$(es DELETE "/_snapshot/minio-repo")
echo "  Response: ${RESULT}"

echo "  Verifying repository is gone:"
REPOS=$(es GET "/_snapshot")
if echo "${REPOS}" | grep -q 'minio-repo'; then
  warn "minio-repo still listed after deletion — check response above."
  exit 1
else
  ok "Snapshot repository minio-repo successfully deleted."
fi

# ─── summary ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

step "API rollback complete"
ok "app-data index routing.allocation.require.zone removed"
ok "Snapshot repository minio-repo deleted"
echo
echo "Remaining manual rollback steps (require pod restarts):"
echo "  3. Remove node.attr.zone from elasticsearch.yml on all nodes and rolling-restart"
echo "  4. Delete es-secure-settings secret (S3 keystore credentials) and rolling-restart"
echo "  5. Disable xpack.security.http.ssl in elasticsearch.yml and rolling-restart"
echo "  6. Scale down and delete StatefulSet es-data (after step 3 above)"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Rollback non-default CockroachDB configuration to factory defaults.
#
# Cluster state captured: 2026-06-24, namespace cockroachdb, version v24.1.0
#
# NON-DEFAULT SETTINGS AT TIME OF CAPTURE
# ----------------------------------------
# Cluster settings:
#   diagnostics.reporting.enabled = true        (factory default: false)
#   kv.range_merge.queue.enabled   = false       (factory default: true)
#   kv.snapshot_rebalance.max_rate = 208 MiB    (factory default: 32 MiB)
#
# Zone configs:
#   RANGE default gc.ttlseconds    = 14400       (factory default: 90000)
#   TABLE defaultdb.tenant_b.table_c             (user-applied; factory default: none)
#   TABLE defaultdb.tenant_b.table_d             (user-applied; factory default: none)
#
# USAGE (execute only in the approved change window):
#   bash rollback.sh
#
# PREREQUISITES:
#   - kubectl configured with access to the cockroachdb namespace
#   - CockroachDB TLS certs mounted at /cockroach/cockroach-certs inside pods

set -euo pipefail

NAMESPACE=cockroachdb
POD=crdb-cluster-0
CERTS_DIR=/cockroach/cockroach-certs

sql() {
    kubectl -n "$NAMESPACE" exec "$POD" -- \
        ./cockroach sql --certs-dir="$CERTS_DIR" -e "$1"
}

echo "========================================================"
echo " CockroachDB Rollback — Revert Non-Default Settings"
echo " Namespace : $NAMESPACE"
echo " Pod       : $POD"
echo "========================================================"
echo ""

# ── Step 1: Cluster settings ─────────────────────────────────
echo "--- Step 1: Reset cluster settings to factory defaults ---"

echo "  [1/3] diagnostics.reporting.enabled  false  (was: true)"
sql "SET CLUSTER SETTING diagnostics.reporting.enabled = false;"

echo "  [2/3] kv.range_merge.queue.enabled   true   (was: false)"
sql "SET CLUSTER SETTING kv.range_merge.queue.enabled = true;"

# Use bytes to avoid any locale/unit ambiguity; 32 MiB = 33554432 bytes.
echo "  [3/3] kv.snapshot_rebalance.max_rate 33554432 bytes (32 MiB, was: 208 MiB)"
sql "SET CLUSTER SETTING kv.snapshot_rebalance.max_rate = 33554432;"

echo ""

# ── Step 2: Zone configurations ───────────────────────────────
echo "--- Step 2: Revert zone configurations ---"

# RANGE default gc.ttlseconds was reduced to 14400 (4 h); restore to 90000 (25 h).
# Only gc.ttlseconds is overridden here; range_min/max_bytes and num_replicas
# are already at their v24.1 factory values (128 MiB / 512 MiB / 3).
echo "  [1/3] RANGE default gc.ttlseconds    90000  (was: 14400)"
sql "ALTER RANGE default CONFIGURE ZONE USING gc.ttlseconds = 90000;"

# User-applied zone configs on application tables — discard entirely so these
# tables inherit replication settings from RANGE default.
echo "  [2/3] Discard zone config for TABLE defaultdb.tenant_b.table_c"
sql "ALTER TABLE defaultdb.tenant_b.table_c CONFIGURE ZONE DISCARD;"

echo "  [3/3] Discard zone config for TABLE defaultdb.tenant_b.table_d"
sql "ALTER TABLE defaultdb.tenant_b.table_d CONFIGURE ZONE DISCARD;"

echo ""

# ── Step 3: Verification ──────────────────────────────────────
echo "--- Step 3: Post-rollback verification ---"

echo ""
echo "  Non-default cluster settings (expect only cluster.secret and version):"
sql "SELECT variable, value, default_value
     FROM crdb_internal.cluster_settings
     WHERE value != default_value
     ORDER BY variable;"

echo ""
echo "  Zone config for RANGE default (gc.ttlseconds should be 90000):"
sql "SHOW ZONE CONFIGURATION FOR RANGE default;"

echo ""
echo "  Tenant-B table zone configs (both rows should be absent after discard):"
sql "SELECT target FROM crdb_internal.zones WHERE target LIKE '%tenant_b%';"

echo ""
echo "========================================================"
echo " Rollback complete."
echo "========================================================"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# rollback.sh — Revert non-default Elasticsearch settings to cluster defaults
#
# Audit date: 2026-06-25
# Cluster: elasticsearch namespace, es-http service (HTTPS, port 9200)
#
# NON-DEFAULT SETTINGS IDENTIFIED
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# API-revertible (handled in steps 1–2 below):
#   1. index app-data: index.routing.allocation.require.zone = "new"
#      (pins all shards to nodes with attribute zone=new; no such constraint by default)
#   2. snapshot repository "minio-repo"
#      (type=s3, endpoint=minio.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local:9000, bucket=es-backups)
#      (no snapshot repositories exist by default)
#
# Requires manual action (elasticsearch.yml change + rolling restart):
#   3. Node attributes — zone=original on es-{0,1,2}, zone=new on es-data-{0,1}
#      set via node.attr.zone in elasticsearch.yml; cannot be changed via API.
#      → Remove node.attr.zone from each pod's config and perform a rolling restart.
#   4. S3 keystore credentials — s3.client.default.access_key / s3.client.default.secret_key
#      loaded from the es-secure-settings Kubernetes secret into the Elasticsearch keystore.
#      They become harmless orphans once minio-repo is deleted (step 2) but persist
#      in the keystore until a rolling restart after deleting the secret:
#        kubectl -n elasticsearch delete secret es-secure-settings
#      then rolling-restart the StatefulSet(s).
#   5. TLS/HTTPS — cluster runs xpack.security.http.ssl (HTTPS on port 9200).
#      This is configured in elasticsearch.yml, not via API.
#      → Remove xpack.security.http.ssl settings from each node's config and rolling-restart.
#   6. Scale — StatefulSet es-data (2 replicas, zone=new data nodes) was added.
#      Remove AFTER reverting step 1 (otherwise shards pinned to zone=new go unassigned):
#        kubectl -n elasticsearch scale statefulset es-data --replicas=0
#        kubectl -n elasticsearch delete statefulset es-data
#        kubectl -n elasticsearch delete service es-data-headless
#
# WHAT IS ALREADY AT DEFAULT (no action needed)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#   • Cluster-level persistent/transient settings: verified empty via /_cluster/settings
#   • index test-index: only _tier_preference=data_content (automatic Elasticsearch default)
#   • ILM policies: all built-in managed policies (managed=true in _meta)
#   • Composable index templates: all built-in managed templates
#   • Component templates: all built-in managed templates
#   • Ingest pipelines: all built-in managed pipelines
#
# PREREQUISITES
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#   Run from inside the cluster (or via kubectl port-forward es-http 9200:9200).
#   Override ES_URL, ES_USER, or ES_PASSWORD via environment variables if needed.
#
# SAFETY
#   This script does NOT modify or delete any index data. It only removes
#   configuration overlays applied on top of Elasticsearch defaults.

set -euo pipefail

ES_URL="${ES_URL:-https://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200}"
ES_USER="${ES_USER:-elastic}"
ES_PASSWORD="${ES_PASSWORD:-elasticpass-elastic-password-next}"

# ─── helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

es() {
  local method="$1"
  local path="$2"
  local body="${3:-}"
  if [ -n "$body" ]; then
    curl -sk -u "${ES_USER}:${ES_PASSWORD}" \
      -X "${method}" "${ES_URL}${path}" \
      -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d "${body}"
  else
    curl -sk -u "${ES_USER}:${ES_PASSWORD}" \
      -X "${method}" "${ES_URL}${path}"
  fi
  printf '\n'
}

step() { printf '\n──── %s ────\n' "$*"; }
ok()   { printf '  ✓ %s\n' "$*"; }
warn() { printf '  ! %s\n' "$*"; }

# ─── verify connectivity ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

step "Verifying connectivity to ${ES_URL}"
HEALTH=$(es GET "/_cluster/health?timeout=10s")
STATUS=$(printf '%s' "${HEALTH}" | grep -o '"status":"[^"]*"' | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f4)
ok "Cluster health: ${STATUS}"

# ─── step 1: remove custom shard routing requirement from app-data ─────────────
#
# Observed:  index.routing.allocation.require.zone = "new"
# Default:   no require constraint; ES uses tier preference and balance heuristics
# Effect of removal: shards on app-data become eligible for placement on any
#   data-eligible node, not just those with zone=new (es-data-{0,1}).
#   After this step it is safe to scale down es-data (see manual step 6 above).

step "1/2  Remove routing.allocation.require.zone from index 'app-data'"
printf '  Current setting (before change):\n'
BEFORE=$(es GET "/app-data/_settings?flat_settings=true")
printf '  %s\n' "${BEFORE}" | grep 'require.zone' || printf '  (already absent)\n'

printf '  Sending PUT /app-data/_settings ...\n'
RESULT=$(es PUT "/app-data/_settings" '{"index.routing.allocation.require.zone": null}')
printf '  Response: %s\n' "${RESULT}"

AFTER=$(es GET "/app-data/_settings?flat_settings=true")
if printf '%s' "${AFTER}" | grep -q 'routing.allocation.require.zone'; then
  warn "Setting still present after removal — check the response above."
  exit 1
fi
ok "index.routing.allocation.require.zone removed from app-data."

# ─── step 2: delete custom MinIO snapshot repository ─────────────────────────
#
# Observed:  minio-repo (type=s3, endpoint=minio.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local:9000,

# Default:   no snapshot repositories
# Note:      This does not delete any snapshots stored in the bucket; the objects

#            registration of it.

step "2/2  Delete snapshot repository 'minio-repo'"
printf '  Checking for in-progress snapshots ...\n'
SNAPS=$(es GET "/_snapshot/minio-repo/_current")
if printf '%s' "${SNAPS}" | grep -q '"snapshot"'; then
  warn "In-progress snapshot detected — aborting to avoid corruption."
  warn "Wait for the snapshot to complete (or cancel it), then re-run."
  exit 1
fi
ok "No in-progress snapshots."

printf '  Sending DELETE /_snapshot/minio-repo ...\n'
RESULT=$(es DELETE "/_snapshot/minio-repo")
printf '  Response: %s\n' "${RESULT}"

if printf '%s' "${REPOS}" | grep -q 'minio-repo'; then
  warn "minio-repo still listed after deletion — check the response above."
  exit 1
fi
ok "Snapshot repository minio-repo deleted."

# ─── summary ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

step "API rollback complete"
ok "app-data: routing.allocation.require.zone constraint removed"
ok "Snapshot repository minio-repo deleted"

printf 'Remaining manual rollback steps (each requires elasticsearch.yml edits + rolling restart):\n'
printf '  3. Remove node.attr.zone from elasticsearch.yml on all nodes\n'
printf '     (affects es-{0,1,2} with zone=original and es-data-{0,1} with zone=new)\n'
printf '  4. Delete the es-secure-settings secret and rolling-restart to purge S3 keystore entries\n'

printf '  5. Disable xpack.security.http.ssl in elasticsearch.yml and rolling-restart\n'
printf '  6. Scale down and delete the es-data StatefulSet (zone=new data nodes):\n'
printf '       kubectl -n elasticsearch scale statefulset es-data --replicas=0\n'
printf '       kubectl -n elasticsearch delete statefulset es-data service es-data-headless\n'

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# rollback.sh — Revert non-default Elasticsearch settings to cluster defaults
#
# Audit date: 2026-06-25
# Cluster: elasticsearch namespace, es-http service (HTTPS, port 9200)
#
# NON-DEFAULT SETTINGS IDENTIFIED
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# API-revertible (handled in steps 1–2 below):
#   1. index app-data: index.routing.allocation.require.zone = "new"
#      (pins all shards to nodes with attribute zone=new; no such constraint by default)
#   2. snapshot repository "minio-repo"
#      (type=s3, endpoint=minio.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local:9000, bucket=es-backups)
#      (no snapshot repositories exist by default)
#
# Requires manual action (elasticsearch.yml change + rolling restart):
#   3. Node attributes — zone=original on es-{0,1,2}, zone=new on es-data-{0,1}
#      set via node.attr.zone in elasticsearch.yml; cannot be changed via API.
#      → Remove node.attr.zone from each pod's config and perform a rolling restart.
#   4. S3 keystore credentials — s3.client.default.access_key / s3.client.default.secret_key
#      loaded from the es-secure-settings Kubernetes secret into the Elasticsearch keystore.
#      They become harmless orphans once minio-repo is deleted (step 2) but persist
#      in the keystore until a rolling restart after deleting the secret:
#        kubectl -n elasticsearch delete secret es-secure-settings
#      then rolling-restart the StatefulSet(s).
#   5. TLS/HTTPS — cluster runs xpack.security.http.ssl (HTTPS on port 9200).
#      This is configured in elasticsearch.yml, not via API.
#      → Remove xpack.security.http.ssl settings from each node's config and rolling-restart.
#   6. Scale — StatefulSet es-data (2 replicas, zone=new data nodes) was added.
#      Remove AFTER reverting step 1 (otherwise shards pinned to zone=new go unassigned):
#        kubectl -n elasticsearch scale statefulset es-data --replicas=0
#        kubectl -n elasticsearch delete statefulset es-data
#        kubectl -n elasticsearch delete service es-data-headless
#
# WHAT IS ALREADY AT DEFAULT (no action needed)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#   • Cluster-level persistent/transient settings: verified empty via /_cluster/settings
#   • index test-index: only _tier_preference=data_content (automatic Elasticsearch default)
#   • ILM policies: all built-in managed policies (managed=true in _meta)
#   • Composable index templates: all built-in managed templates
#   • Component templates: all built-in managed templates
#   • Ingest pipelines: all built-in managed pipelines
#
# PREREQUISITES
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#   Run from inside the cluster (or via kubectl port-forward es-http 9200:9200).
#   Override ES_URL, ES_USER, or ES_PASSWORD via environment variables if needed.
#
# SAFETY
#   This script does NOT modify or delete any index data. It only removes
#   configuration overlays applied on top of Elasticsearch defaults.

set -euo pipefail

ES_URL="${ES_URL:-https://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200}"
ES_USER="${ES_USER:-elastic}"
ES_PASSWORD="${ES_PASSWORD:-elasticpass-elastic-password-next}"

# ─── helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

es() {
  local method="$1"
  local path="$2"
  local body="${3:-}"
  if [ -n "$body" ]; then
    curl -sk -u "${ES_USER}:${ES_PASSWORD}" \
      -X "${method}" "${ES_URL}${path}" \
      -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d "${body}"
  else
    curl -sk -u "${ES_USER}:${ES_PASSWORD}" \
      -X "${method}" "${ES_URL}${path}"
  fi
  printf '\n'
}

step() { printf '\n──── %s ────\n' "$*"; }
ok()   { printf '  ✓ %s\n' "$*"; }
warn() { printf '  ! %s\n' "$*"; }

# ─── verify connectivity ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

step "Verifying connectivity to ${ES_URL}"
HEALTH=$(es GET "/_cluster/health?timeout=10s")
STATUS=$(printf '%s' "${HEALTH}" | grep -o '"status":"[^"]*"' | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f4)
ok "Cluster health: ${STATUS}"

# ─── step 1: remove custom shard routing requirement from app-data ─────────────
#
# Observed:  index.routing.allocation.require.zone = "new"
# Default:   no require constraint; ES uses tier preference and balance heuristics
# Effect of removal: shards on app-data become eligible for placement on any
#   data-eligible node, not just those with zone=new (es-data-{0,1}).
#   After this step it is safe to scale down es-data (see manual step 6 above).

step "1/2  Remove routing.allocation.require.zone from index 'app-data'"
printf '  Current setting (before change):\n'
BEFORE=$(es GET "/app-data/_settings?flat_settings=true")
printf '  %s\n' "${BEFORE}" | grep 'require.zone' || printf '  (already absent)\n'

printf '  Sending PUT /app-data/_settings ...\n'
RESULT=$(es PUT "/app-data/_settings" '{"index.routing.allocation.require.zone": null}')
printf '  Response: %s\n' "${RESULT}"

AFTER=$(es GET "/app-data/_settings?flat_settings=true")
if printf '%s' "${AFTER}" | grep -q 'routing.allocation.require.zone'; then
  warn "Setting still present after removal — check the response above."
  exit 1
fi
ok "index.routing.allocation.require.zone removed from app-data."

# ─── step 2: delete custom MinIO snapshot repository ─────────────────────────
#
# Observed:  minio-repo (type=s3, endpoint=minio.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local:9000,
#            bucket=es-backups, path_style_access=true, protocol=http)
# Default:   no snapshot repositories
# Note:      This does not delete any snapshots stored in the bucket; the objects
#            in MinIO remain intact. Deleting the repository only removes ES's
#            registration of it.

step "2/2  Delete snapshot repository 'minio-repo'"
printf '  Checking for in-progress snapshots ...\n'
SNAPS=$(es GET "/_snapshot/minio-repo/_current")
if printf '%s' "${SNAPS}" | grep -q '"snapshot"'; then
  warn "In-progress snapshot detected — aborting to avoid corruption."
  warn "Wait for the snapshot to complete (or cancel it), then re-run."
  exit 1
fi
ok "No in-progress snapshots."

printf '  Sending DELETE /_snapshot/minio-repo ...\n'
RESULT=$(es DELETE "/_snapshot/minio-repo")
printf '  Response: %s\n' "${RESULT}"

REPOS=$(es GET "/_snapshot")
if printf '%s' "${REPOS}" | grep -q 'minio-repo'; then
  warn "minio-repo still listed after deletion — check the response above."
  exit 1
fi
ok "Snapshot repository minio-repo deleted."

# ─── summary ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

step "API rollback complete"
ok "app-data: routing.allocation.require.zone constraint removed"
ok "Snapshot repository minio-repo deleted"
printf '\n'
printf 'Remaining manual rollback steps (each requires elasticsearch.yml edits + rolling restart):\n'
printf '  3. Remove node.attr.zone from elasticsearch.yml on all nodes\n'
printf '     (affects es-{0,1,2} with zone=original and es-data-{0,1} with zone=new)\n'
printf '  4. Delete the es-secure-settings secret and rolling-restart to purge S3 keystore entries\n'
printf '       kubectl -n elasticsearch delete secret es-secure-settings\n'
printf '  5. Disable xpack.security.http.ssl in elasticsearch.yml and rolling-restart\n'
printf '  6. Scale down and delete the es-data StatefulSet (zone=new data nodes):\n'
printf '       kubectl -n elasticsearch scale statefulset es-data --replicas=0\n'
printf '       kubectl -n elasticsearch delete statefulset es-data service es-data-headless\n'

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Rollback non-default CockroachDB configuration to factory defaults.
#
# Cluster state captured: 2026-06-24, namespace cockroachdb, version v24.1.0
#
# NON-DEFAULT SETTINGS AT TIME OF CAPTURE
# ----------------------------------------
# Cluster settings:
#   diagnostics.reporting.enabled = true        (factory default: false)
#   kv.range_merge.queue.enabled   = false       (factory default: true)
#   kv.snapshot_rebalance.max_rate = 208 MiB    (factory default: 32 MiB)
#
# Zone configs:
#   RANGE default gc.ttlseconds    = 14400       (factory default: 90000)
#   TABLE defaultdb.tenant_b.table_c             (user-applied; factory default: none)
#   TABLE defaultdb.tenant_b.table_d             (user-applied; factory default: none)
#
# USAGE (execute only in the approved change window):
#   bash rollback.sh
#
# PREREQUISITES:
#   - kubectl configured with access to the cockroachdb namespace
#   - CockroachDB TLS certs mounted at /cockroach/cockroach-certs inside pods

set -euo pipefail

NAMESPACE=cockroachdb
POD=crdb-cluster-0
CERTS_DIR=/cockroach/cockroach-certs

sql() {
    kubectl -n "$NAMESPACE" exec "$POD" -- \
        ./cockroach sql --certs-dir="$CERTS_DIR" -e "$1"
}

echo "========================================================"
echo " CockroachDB Rollback — Revert Non-Default Settings"
echo " Namespace : $NAMESPACE"
echo " Pod       : $POD"
echo "========================================================"
echo ""

# ── Step 1: Cluster settings ─────────────────────────────────
echo "--- Step 1: Reset cluster settings to factory defaults ---"

echo "  [1/3] diagnostics.reporting.enabled  false  (was: true)"
sql "SET CLUSTER SETTING diagnostics.reporting.enabled = false;"

echo "  [2/3] kv.range_merge.queue.enabled   true   (was: false)"
sql "SET CLUSTER SETTING kv.range_merge.queue.enabled = true;"

# Use bytes to avoid any locale/unit ambiguity; 32 MiB = 33554432 bytes.
echo "  [3/3] kv.snapshot_rebalance.max_rate 33554432 bytes (32 MiB, was: 208 MiB)"
sql "SET CLUSTER SETTING kv.snapshot_rebalance.max_rate = 33554432;"

echo ""

# ── Step 2: Zone configurations ───────────────────────────────
echo "--- Step 2: Revert zone configurations ---"

# RANGE default gc.ttlseconds was reduced to 14400 (4 h); restore to 90000 (25 h).
# Only gc.ttlseconds is overridden here; range_min/max_bytes and num_replicas
# are already at their v24.1 factory values (128 MiB / 512 MiB / 3).
echo "  [1/3] RANGE default gc.ttlseconds    90000  (was: 14400)"
sql "ALTER RANGE default CONFIGURE ZONE USING gc.ttlseconds = 90000;"

# User-applied zone configs on application tables — discard entirely so these
# tables inherit replication settings from RANGE default.
echo "  [2/3] Discard zone config for TABLE defaultdb.tenant_b.table_c"
sql "ALTER TABLE defaultdb.tenant_b.table_c CONFIGURE ZONE DISCARD;"

echo "  [3/3] Discard zone config for TABLE defaultdb.tenant_b.table_d"
sql "ALTER TABLE defaultdb.tenant_b.table_d CONFIGURE ZONE DISCARD;"

echo ""

# ── Step 3: Verification ──────────────────────────────────────
echo "--- Step 3: Post-rollback verification ---"

echo ""
echo "  Non-default cluster settings (expect only cluster.secret and version):"
sql "SELECT variable, value, default_value
     FROM crdb_internal.cluster_settings
     WHERE value != default_value
     ORDER BY variable;"

echo ""
echo "  Zone config for RANGE default (gc.ttlseconds should be 90000):"
sql "SHOW ZONE CONFIGURATION FOR RANGE default;"

echo ""
echo "  Tenant-B table zone configs (both rows should be absent after discard):"
sql "SELECT target FROM crdb_internal.zones WHERE target LIKE '%tenant_b%';"

echo ""
echo "========================================================"
echo " Rollback complete."
echo "========================================================"
